gnar.d:
n. A knot or gnarl in wood; hence, a tough, thickset man; --
   written also gnarr.
v. i. To gnarl; to snarl; to growl; -- written also gnarr.

gnarl.t:
n. 木节, 木瘤
vi. 形成木节, 咆哮
vt. 扭曲
gnarl.d: v. twist into a state of deformity

gnarled.d: s. used of old persons or old trees; covered with knobs or knots

gnarliest.t: adj. gnarly（ 多瘤的，粗糙的）的变形

gnarling.t:
n. 木节；木瘤
vi. 咆哮；形成木节
gnarling.d: p. pr. & vb. n. of Gnarl

(gnarl 的第三人称 -s形式) n. 木节, 木瘤
vi. 形成木节, 咆哮
vt. 扭曲

-s form of gnarl
v. twist into a state of deformity

gnarly.d: s used of old persons or old trees; covered with knobs or knots

vi. 咬牙切齿；磨牙；(上下牙)互相叩击

n. (British usage) mosquito

gnatcatcher.d: n. very small North American and South American warblers

a. 颚的, 颌的

gnathion.d: n the most inferior point of the mandible in the midline

gnathite.d:
n. Any one of the mouth appendages of the Arthropoda. They
   are known as mandibles, maxillae, and maxillipeds.

gnathology.t: 咀嚼器学; 咀嚼器学

gnathonic.t: a. 阿谀的, 谄媚的

gnathopodite.d:
n. Any leglike appendage of a crustacean, when modified
   wholly, or in part, to serve as a jaw, esp. one of the maxillipeds.

gnathostoma.d:
n. pl. A comprehensive division of vertebrates, including
   all that have distinct jaws, in contrast with the leptocardians and
   marsipobranchs (Cyclostoma), which lack them.

gnathostomata.d: n. comprising all vertebrates with upper and lower jaws

gnathostome.d: n. a vertebrate animal possessing true jaws

gnathotheca.d: n. The horney covering of the lower mandible of a bird.

v bite or chew on with the teeth

   granite and gneiss, or mica slate and gneiss.

gneissose.d: a. Having the structure of gneiss.

a. Sententious; uttering or containing maxims, or striking
   detached thoughts; aphoristic.
a. Gnomonical.

gnomonic.t: a. 表的； 用表测时的
